name: Andorra
experiences: Andorra la Vella old town and Caldea
Ordino heritage museums and valley walks
Grandvalira skiing or summer mountain viewpoints
Encamp Funicamp access and lake hikes
avoid: Christmas–New Year and February school-holiday ski weeks if you dislike surge pricing and busy slopes.
April and November shoulder periods when some lifts, mountain services and seasonal businesses shut between ski and hiking seasons.
Driving old town cores at peak shopping hours — parking is easier than street navigation, and buses are often less stressful.
stopoverNote: Andorra is small enough for a long weekend, but 4 days is the sweet spot if you want the capital, one heritage valley, one Grandvalira day and one funicular or adventure-park day without rushing.

cities.name: Andorra la Vella
cities.notes: Compact capital for historic lanes, shopping boulevards, spa time at Caldea and the easiest base for bus connections across the principality.

cities.name: Ordino
cities.notes: Andorra's prettiest heritage village area, with manor-house museums, stone lanes and quick access to summer trailheads or winter mountain roads.

cities.name: Canillo
cities.notes: Grandvalira gateway for skiing in winter, mountain viewpoints in summer and classic Andorran postcard stops like Sant Joan de Caselles.

cities.name: Encamp
cities.notes: Practical valley town with the Funicamp gondola, Engolasters access and an easy final-night base before driving or busing out of Andorra.

itinerary.sim: Use an EU eSIM only if it explicitly includes Andorra roaming; many Spain/France plans charge extra here. Download offline maps before arrival and expect the simplest all-trip setup from a roaming-enabled Eurolink-style eSIM.

Andorra la Vella.name: Andorra la Vella
Andorra la Vella.note: Start in the capital for historic sights and spa time; keep a second day here only if you want Caldea plus the Naturland adventure-park detour south of the city.
experiences: Andorra la Vella old town and Caldea

days.theme: Old Town, parliament history and Caldea evening

activities.name: Casa de la Vall
activities.tip: Adult admission is €5; guided visits are a €1.50 supplement, so book ahead if you want an English-language slot.

activities.name: Sant Esteve Church, Barri Antic lanes and Plaça del Poble walk
La Noblesse du Temps (Dalí clock) and Meritxell Avenue stroll
Caldea Classic 3-hour spa session
activities.tip: Official Classic entry starts from €34.50 and varies by slot; evening sessions usually feel best after sightseeing.

activities.name: Naturland Aventura Plus day pass
activities.tip: Official advance adult price is €45, or €48 at the gate; go early if you want Tobotronc plus other rides without queues.

activities.name: Drive or bus south to Sant Julià de Lòria for Naturland
activities.cost: ₹165–₹300 pp by interurban bus or ₹1,800–₹2,700 by taxi

activities.name: Return to Andorra la Vella for shopping or a shorter repeat Caldea slot

Ordino.name: Ordino
Ordino.note: Ordino is ideal for one slow heritage-and-landscape day: museum manor house, village lunch and a simple valley walk without constant transfers.
experiences: Ordino heritage museums and valley walks

days.theme: Heritage village, manor house and valley viewpoints

activities.name: Casa d'Areny-Plandolit
activities.tip: Adult admission is typically €5; pair it with the village museum circuit if you enjoy local history.

activities.name: Ordino old village lanes and Sant Corneli i Sant Cebrià church
Ruta del Ferro riverside section or Segudet viewpoint walk
Coll d'Ordino scenic stop or short drive toward Ordino Arcalís

hotels.name: Hotel Santa Bàrbara de la Vall d'Ordino

Canillo.name: Canillo
Canillo.note: Give Canillo the dedicated mountain day: ski Grandvalira from December to March, or swap to viewpoints, churches and easy alpine stops in summer.
experiences: Grandvalira skiing or summer mountain viewpoints

activities.name: Grandvalira adult day ski pass
activities.tip: Official adult day-pass pricing is dynamic and starts around €42.50 online; peak holiday dates can cost more, so book early.

activities.name: Sant Joan de Caselles church exterior stop
Mirador Roc del Quer
activities.tip: Individual adult entry is €6; parking is free, but the ticket does not include bus transport.

activities.name: Meritxell Sanctuary detour on the return

Encamp.name: Encamp
Encamp.note: Finish in Encamp if you want a gentler final day with gondola views, a museum stop and an easy lake walk before departure.
experiences: Encamp Funicamp access and lake hikes

days.theme: Funicamp, auto museum and lake walk

activities.name: Funicamp round trip
activities.tip: Summer round-trip price is €15; in winter it functions mainly as Grandvalira access rather than a sightseeing ride.

activities.name: Museu Nacional de l'Automòbil
activities.tip: General admission is €5; add the €1.50 guided supplement only if you want extra context on the classic-car collection.

activities.name: Sant Romà de les Bons complex and lookout

connections.method: Use the interurban cooperative bus network northbound, or drive/taxi if you want a faster hotel-to-hotel transfer.
connections.cost: ₹165–₹300 pp by bus or roughly ₹1,800–₹2,700 by taxi

connections.method: Driving is the simplest direct move; by public transport you usually connect back through the central valley bus corridor.
connections.cost: ₹300–₹560 pp by bus depending on zones or roughly ₹3,600–₹4,950 by taxi

connections.method: Short valley hop by interurban bus, taxi or rental car down the CG-2 corridor.
connections.cost: ₹165–₹300 pp by bus or roughly ₹1,350–₹2,250 by taxi

extras: Andorra has no rail network, so plan around interurban buses, short taxi hops or a rental car if you want maximum flexibility for viewpoints and adventure parks.
The cooperative bus system uses zone-based single fares: roughly €1.85, €3.35, €4.70 and €6.20 depending on distance, which is why short hops are cheap but cross-country rides add up.
If you are skiing, stay close to your chosen sector the previous night; morning parking and weather are far easier when you are already in Canillo or Encamp.
For June–September trips, swap ski time for hiking, Funicamp viewpoints and longer Ordino valley walks; the same city order still works well.
